Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No tentative; parties to appear. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: PALMISANO DELIVERY SERVICE OF SAN FRANCISCO, LLC. VS. WHEELHOUSE SOLUTIONS, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File X-Complt
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 4, 2014, Line 7, DEFENDANTS FREEMAN DECORATING SERVICES, INC., CHAMPION EXPOSITION SERVICES, LLC, WCP EXPOSITIONS OPERATING COMPANY, LLC'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Cross-Complaint. Defendant's Motion for Leave to File a Cross-Complaint is granted. Cross-complaints may be brought against any person from whom Defendant seeks equitable indemnity, and need only allege that the harm for which Defendant is being sued is attributable, at least in part, to the cross-defendant. American Motorcycle Ass'n v. Sup. Ct (1978) 20 Cal.3d 578, 607. The interaction of the partial indemnity doctrine with the cross-complaint procedures works no undue prejudice to the rights of plaintiffs. Id. at 606. Moreover, leave to file a permissive cross-complaint may be granted "in the interest of justice" at any time during the course of the action. CCP § 428.50(c). Defendant claims that, due to newly learned facts, the potential cross-defendants may be liable to Defendant for indemnity and/or contribution. Moreover, Defendant provided a reasonable excuse for the delay in acquiring these facts-i.e., the potential prejudice Defendant would have faced had the deposition of Palmisano occurred prior to the end of 2013. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: SUSAN KOOPMAN VS. RED AND WHITE FLEET et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 4, 2014, Line 9, PLAINTIFF SUSAN KOOPMAN'S Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Complaint. The motion is denied. The Court assumes it has jurisdiction to entertain this motion because a judgment of dismissal has never been entered in favor of defendant. Nevertheless, the Court denies amendment because plaintiff has unreasonably delayed bringing the motion and granting amendment will prejudice defendant. Paragraph 7 of Mr. Goodroe's declaration indicates that plaintiff knew or should have known of the training issue on November 5, 2013. Plaintiff should have sought leave to amend at or near that time. Plaintiff did not properly seek leave to amend until filing this motion on February 20, 2014. The delay is prejudicial because plaintiff is attempting to change its theory of recovery against defendant and defendant will necessarily be obligated to incur new discovery costs. "There is a platoon of authority to the effect that a long unexcused delay is sufficient to uphold a trial judge's decision to deny the opportunity to amend pleadings, particularly where the new amendment would interject a new issue which requires further discovery." Green v. Rancho Santa Margarita Mort. Co. (1994) 28 Cal.App.4th 686, 692. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: BULLIVANT HOUSER BAILEY PC VS. YASHENG GROUP
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 4, 2014, Line 19, PLAINTIFF BULLIVANT HOUSER BAILEY PC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Granted. Plaintiff has met its burden of producing admissible evidence on each element of the cause of action for account stated. (CCP sec. 437c(p)(1).) The elements of a cause of action for account stated are: (1) Previous transactions between the parties establishing the relationship of debtor and creditor; (2) an agreement between the parties, express or implied, on the amount due from the debtor to the creditor; and (3) a promise by the debtor, express or implied, to pay the amount due. (Zinn v. Fred R. Bright Co. (1969) 271 Cal.App.2d 597, 600.) As to the first element, Plaintiff has produced admissible evidence showing that the parties entered into an engagement letter setting forth payment terms. (Declaration of Edward Winchester, Ex. A.) Plaintiff has also produced evidence establishing that Defendant paid the first two invoices and partially made payment on a third. (Declaration of Ron Kinonen, par. 3.) As to the second element, an agreement between parties to establish an account stated "comes about by the creditor rendering a statement of the account to the debtor." (Zinn, 271 Cal.App.2d at 600.) Plaintiff has produced evidence showing Plaintiff regularly sent invoices to Defendant. (Kinonen Decl., par. 2, Ex. E; Supplemental Declaration of Ron Kinonen, par. 2-3, exs. I-L.) As to the third element, Plaintiff has produced admissible evidence showing that Defendant did not object to the bills (except for one which the parties agreed to settle the amount) by writing within 30 days of receipt, as required by the engagement letter. (Kinonen Decl., par. 4.) "When a statement is rendered to a debtor and no reply is made in a reasonable time, the law implies an agreement that the account is correct as rendered." (Maggio, Inc. v. Neal (1987) 196 Cal.App.3d 745, 753.) The evidence also establishes that Defendant agreed to settle the disputed bill for $33,036. (Kinonen Decl., par. 5, Ex. F.) Consequently, the burden shifted to Defendant to establish a triable issue of material fact. (CCP sec. 437c(p)(1).) Defendant has not met its burden. Defendant has not produced admissible evidence showing that it objected to the invoices in writing within 30 days of receipt, as required by the engagement letter. Defendant has not produced admissible evidence showing that it entered into the settlement agreement under duress. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No tentative; parties to appear. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: DRIP DROP INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. SOS HYDRATION INC., et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 4, 2014, Line 20, DEFENDANT SOS HYDRATION INC., BLANCA LIZAOLA AND JAMES MAYO'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The Court sustains the demurrer without leave to amend as to causes of action 3, 4, 11, 14, 15, 16, 19, and 21. The California Uniform Trade Secrets Act preempts/displaces those claims. Those claims are based on the same nucleus of facts that underpin the statutory misappropriation of trade secret cause of action. Plaintiff's reference to non-trade-secret-proprietary-confidential information does not save the causes of action. California does not recognize a property right in "confidential" or "proprietary" information that does not meet the statutory definition of a trade secret. See Silvaco Data Sys. v. Intel Corp., (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 210, 239 fn.22 (overruled on other grounds in Kwikset Corp. v. Superior Court, (2011) 51 Cal.4th 310). The Court sustains the demurrer without leave to amend as to causes of action 17, 18, and 20. Those statutory claims do not expressly allude to trade secrets in a particular setting. See Silvaco Data, 184 Cal.App.4th at 241. The Court sustains the demurrer without leave to amend as to causes of action 6, 8, and 10. California does not recognize an aiding and abetting breach of contract theory of liability. Plaintiff's remedy is to seek leave to plead an interference with contract or prospective advantage cause of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: HYE-YOUNG SHIN VS. NATIONAL SURGICAL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 4, 2014, Line 21, DEFENDANT NATIONAL SURGICAL HOSPITALS, INC.'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. Defendant National Surgical Hospitals Inc.'s motion to quash service of summons is granted pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure sections 418.10 and 581(h). Plaintiff has not met her burden of establishing minimum contacts between Defendant National Surgical Hospitals, Inc. and California for this Court to exercise personal jurisdiction over the Defendant. Defendant National Surgical Hospitals, Inc. is dismissed from this action without prejudice. rulings.Case Title: MICHELE D AMICO et al VS. FERDINANDO BALLARE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 4, 2014, Line 23, PLAINTIFFS MICHELE D AMICO AND SIDNEA D AMICO'S Motion For Preliminary Injunction. The Court grants plaintiffs' motion for preliminary injunction in part. The Court orders that plaintiffs are restored to their prior positions with South Beach Café, Inc. and defendant is enjoined from interfering with the management of the business unless plaintiffs are removed from their offices in compliance with the Corporations Code. Plaintiffs shall post a $25,000 bond in compliance with CCP 529. rulings.Case Title: MICHELE D AMICO et al VS. FERDINANDO BALLARE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Determine Validity Of Election Of Corporate Officers
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, April 4, 2014, Line 23, PLAINTIFFS MICHELE D AMICO AND SIDNEA D AMICO'S Motion To Determine Validity Of Election Of Corporate Officers. The Court grants plaintiffs' motion to determine the validity of the election of corporate officers. Plaintiffs' present evidence, including tax returns, that indicate they have an ownership interest in South Beach Café, Inc. The evidence also demonstrates that the meeting that occurred on February 20, 2014, did not comport with California Corporations Code secs. 602 and 709.
